home.tagline: Where the Rainforest Meets the Sea
home.subhead: A boutique retreat in Manzanillo, Costa Rica — currently being lovingly restored for your arrival.

about.title: Our Story
about.content:
Ferguson discovered Manzanillo on a solo journey down Costa Rica's southern Caribbean coast — a road that ends at the edge of a wildlife refuge, where the rainforest tumbles into turquoise water and time moves a little differently. What started as a detour became a calling. The hotel had stood for decades, weathered by salt air and tropical rains, but it still had its bones: tall ceilings, wraparound porches, and a garden that seemed determined to grow regardless. Ferguson saw what it could become.

The renovation began in earnest in 2023 — a careful, unhurried process of restoration rather than replacement. Original woodwork is being sanded and refinished. Local artisans are tiling bathrooms by hand. The garden is being coaxed back into something lush and welcoming. We are not rushing. We are doing this right.

Hotel Manzanillo will be a place where guests feel the history of the building, the culture of the community, and the magic of the natural world, all at once.

Our commitment goes beyond the physical structure. Manzanillo is a living, breathing community — one of the most authentic villages on Costa Rica's Caribbean coast. The Afro-Caribbean families who have lived here for generations, the Bribri indigenous communities in the hills, the fishermen who head out before dawn — these are our neighbors, and this hotel exists in relationship with them, not apart from them.

We hire locally. We source locally. We pay above minimum wage. And we maintain our dual-pricing model so that the hotel remains accessible to the community that surrounds it.

We believe that sustainable tourism means more than solar panels and composting — it means building something that the community is proud of, that employs their children, that protects rather than displaces.

We cannot wait to welcome you. Whether you arrive for the reef, the rainforest, the food, or simply the rare quiet of a village that hasn't yet been discovered by the masses — there is a room here with your name on it, a hammock on the porch, and a community worth knowing.

pricing.title: Our Pricing Philosophy
pricing.content:
Manzanillo is a community, not a backdrop. The people who call this village home — the Afro-Caribbean families whose grandparents cleared this land and built these houses, the Bribri indigenous communities whose relationship with this territory spans thousands of years, the fishermen who leave before dawn and the women who cook the best food you've ever eaten — they were here before tourism, and they deserve to remain.

We operate a transparent dual-pricing model. Costa Rican residents and local community members pay one rate. International visitors and foreign tourists pay a higher rate. We are not ashamed of this, and we do not hide it.

The honest explanation is simple: the average international tourist arrives from a country where a daily wage covers what the average local family earns in a week. Charging everyone the same rate sounds equal, but it isn't — it prices the community out of their own village.

Every colón collected at our visitor rate goes directly back into three things: staff wages (we pay above the Costa Rican minimum wage for all positions), community initiatives (we contribute to the local school, the fishing cooperative, and the Bribri cultural center), and the ongoing restoration of the hotel itself.

We believe that tourism, done honestly, should make life better for the people who live where you are visiting. If you are an international visitor and this pricing model gives you pause, we invite you to sit with that discomfort. Then we invite you to come anyway — because Manzanillo will change you, and your visit, at the visitor rate, will change something here too.
